tool.name: Muninn blog_publish
tool.summary: Publish HTML pages to austegard.com via GitHub Pages, optionally update the Atom feed, optionally announce on Bluesky with a follow-up engagement-link commit. Encoded as a flowing DAG so the bsky chain is detached and partial-failure-tolerant.
tool.description: Encoded as a flowing graph: page-commit → wait-for-deploy → (when feed_entry given) feed-update; bsky-announce + engagement-link-commit run as detached side-effects. Caller gets the page URL the moment GH Pages serves it; bsky failures land in `flow.detached_failures` and never bubble up as publish failures. The 300-grapheme bsky cap is enforced as a `validate=` gate BEFORE any createRecord call. Same primary credential as `perch_publish` (GH_TOKEN), same secondary credentials as `bsky_card` (MUNINN_BSKY_HANDLE, MUNINN_BSKY_APP_PASSWORD) — third publishing target after perch_publish and (planned) whtwnd. Issue #5 calls this out as the test for whether v0.4 `writes[]` generalises across multiple publishing surfaces in the same agent.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

env.name: GH_TOKEN
env.prompt: GitHub personal access token. Needs write access to the publish-target repo (oaustegard/austegard.com by default). Same coarse credential as perch_publish, issue_close, perch_triage, verify_patch — share-by-default is intentional. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN if GH_TOKEN is not set.

env.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
env.prompt: Optional fallback for GH_TOKEN. The source reads `os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN') or os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN')`, so either name works. Same scope/sensitivity as GH_TOKEN.

env.name: MUNINN_BSKY_HANDLE
env.prompt: The Bluesky handle to post the announcement as, e.g. 'austegard.com'. Optional — leave blank to skip the bsky chain entirely (the page-publish path still runs).

env.name: MUNINN_BSKY_APP_PASSWORD
env.prompt: App password for the Bluesky handle above. Format is four hyphen-separated four-character groups. Treat as a secret. Required only if MUNINN_BSKY_HANDLE is set; otherwise leave blank. Bsky app passwords cannot be programmatically revoked.

scopes.rationale: Reads the existing feed file (when a feed_entry is supplied), writes the new page commit, optionally commits the updated feed, optionally appends a follow-up engagement-link commit referencing the bsky post. Lands directly on the publish branch with no PR.
When MUNINN_BSKY_HANDLE+PASSWORD are set, creates an app.bsky.feed.post record on the user's repo announcing the published page. Detached side-effect: failure does not abort the publish.
scopes.provider_scope: bsky-app-password (coarse; full repo write except DMs)

scopes.rationale: Talks to api.github.com for git operations, polls the eventually-served page URL on austegard.com to confirm GitHub Pages deploy, and (when bsky configured) talks to bsky.social for the announce.

actions.name: publish_and_announce
actions.summary: Commit a page, wait for GH Pages to serve it, optionally update the Atom feed, optionally announce on Bluesky with a follow-up engagement-link commit.
actions.description: Synchronous nodes: page-commit, wait-for-deploy, feed-update (when feed_entry given). Detached nodes: bsky-announce (when bsky auth supplied), engagement-link-commit (depends on bsky-announce success). The bsky_text input is validated against the 300-grapheme cap (using the bsky_limit utility) BEFORE the bsky chain fires; an over-cap text aborts only the bsky chain, not the page publish. The `deployed` boolean reflects whether the page URL was served within the deploy budget; `bsky_post` is null when the bsky chain was skipped or failed; `detached_failures` lists any background failures.

docs.goal: Publish an HTML page and optionally announce it on Bluesky with engagement linking.
docs.inputs_brief: path (req), content (req), bsky_text (req if announcing), feed_entry (optional), repo (default oaustegard/austegard.com)
docs.outputs_brief: {page_url, commit_sha, feed_sha, deployed, bsky_post, update_sha, detached_failures}

transmits.purpose: publish page commit + feed-update commit + engagement-link commit
announce published page; PDS federates publicly to the network firehose

command:
import os, json, urllib.request
token = os.environ['GH_TOKEN']
req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.github.com/repos/oaustegard/austegard.com', headers={'Authorization': f'token {token}', 'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json', 'User-Agent': 'blog-publish-smoke'})
d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read())
assert d['permissions']['push'], 'token lacks write access to publish-target repo'
print('OK: write access confirmed to', d['name'])

success.stdout_regex: OK: write access confirmed to austegard\.com
